Operation 'getTime' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details, missing_context. The original summary is generic and doesn't specify the API context (Ably). The description lacks clarity about response format, authentication requirements, and practical use cases. Enhanced version specifies the service name, clarifies the numeric return type, notes that no authentication is required, and provides context for when an AI agent should use this operation.

data.current_description: This returns the service time in milliseconds since the epoch.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current time from the Ably service in milliseconds since the Unix epoch. This operation requires no authentication and returns a numeric timestamp value. Useful for synchronizing client-side operations with server time or validating token expiration times.
data.reasoning: The original summary is generic and doesn't specify the API context (Ably). The description lacks clarity about response format, authentication requirements, and practical use cases. Enhanced version specifies the service name, clarifies the numeric return type, notes that no authentication is required, and provides context for when an AI agent should use this operation.

message: Operation 'getMessagesByChannel' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_context. Original description is repetitive and lacks detail. Added information about pagination support, filtering capabilities, return type (Message objects), ordering, and authentication requirements to help AI agents understand the operation's full scope and parameters.

data.current_summary: Get message history for a channel
data.current_description: Get message history for a channel
data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve message history from a channel
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves historical messages from the specified channel. Supports pagination and filtering by time range. Returns an array of Message objects ordered by timestamp. Requires authentication via API key or token.
data.reasoning: Original description is repetitive and lacks detail. Added information about pagination support, filtering capabilities, return type (Message objects), ordering, and authentication requirements to help AI agents understand the operation's full scope and parameters.

message: Operation 'publishMessagesToChannel' has semantic quality issues: missing_input_details, missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs expansion to clarify what the Message schema contains, what gets returned, and authentication requirements. Added details about message delivery to subscribers and server-assigned fields for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_summary: Publish a message to a channel
data.current_description: Publish a message to the specified channel
data.description_suggestion: Publishes a Message object to the specified channel, making it available to all subscribers. Accepts a Message schema with content, encoding, and optional extras (e.g., push notifications). Returns the published message with server-assigned timestamp and ID. Requires authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description needs expansion to clarify what the Message schema contains, what gets returned, and authentication requirements. Added details about message delivery to subscribers and server-assigned fields for better AI agent understanding.

Original description is too vague and doesn't clarify what 'presence' means in this context. Improved summary to be more specific about retrieving member presence. data.summary_suggestion: Retrieve current presence members in a channel
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the current presence state of all members in the specified channel. Returns an array of PresenceMessage objects indicating which clients are currently present. Supports pagination via query parameters. Requires authentication.
data.reasoning: Original description is too vague and doesn't clarify what 'presence' means in this context. Improved summary to be more specific about retrieving member presence. Added details about return type (PresenceMessage objects), pagination support, and authentication to improve AI agent interpretability.

Description incorrectly states 'Get presence on a channel' which is identical to the current presence operation. Clarified that this returns historical events with specific event types, return format, filtering capabilities, and authentication requirements.

data.current_summary: Get presence history of a channel
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves historical presence events for the specified channel, including join, leave, and update events. Returns an array of PresenceMessage objects ordered by timestamp. Supports pagination and time-range filtering. Requires authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and distinct from current presence. Description incorrectly states 'Get presence on a channel' which is identical to the current presence operation. Clarified that this returns historical events with specific event types, return format, filtering capabilities, and authentication requirements.

data.description_suggestion: Retrieves metadata for the specified channel, including channel status, occupancy information, and configuration details. Returns a ChannelDetails object containing current channel state. Requires authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is acceptable but description is repetitive and lacks specificity. data.current_summary: Enumerate all active channels of the application
data.current_description: Enumerate all active channels of the application
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves metadata for all currently active channels in the application. Returns an array of ChannelDetails objects with pagination support. Only includes channels with recent activity. Requires authentication.
data.reasoning: Summary is clear and actionable. Description lacks details about return type, pagination, and what 'active' means. data.current_description: List of device details of devices registed for push notifications.

data.current_summary: Unregister matching devices for push notifications
data.current_description: Unregisters devices. All their subscriptions for receiving push notifications through channels will also be deleted.
data.description_suggestion: Unregisters all devices matching the specified filter criteria. All push notification subscriptions associated with the matched devices will be deleted. Accepts optional query parameters to filter devices by clientId or other criteria. Returns a count of unregistered devices or confirmation of deletion.
data.reasoning: Description lacks clarity on how devices are matched (filter parameters not explained). Missing details about what parameters are accepted and what the response contains. AI agents need to know what query parameters to use to match devices.

data.current_description: Get the full details of a device.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves the complete registration details for a specific device identified by its device ID. Returns a DeviceDetails object containing the device's clientId, metadata, push recipient configuration, and registration status.
data.reasoning: Description is too minimal. AI agents need to know what information is returned (DeviceDetails schema) to determine if this operation provides the data they need. Added schema reference and specific fields returned.

message: Operation 'putPushDeviceDetails' has semantic quality issues: vague_summary, missing_output_details. Summary doesn't clearly indicate upsert behavior. Description lacks clarity on what happens when device doesn't exist and what is returned. Improved summary to reflect upsert semantics and added return value information.

data.current_description: Device registrations can be upserted (the existing registration is replaced entirely) with a PUT operation. Only clientId, metadata and push.recipient are mutable.
data.summary_suggestion: Replace or create a device registration
data.description_suggestion: Replaces an existing device registration entirely (upsert operation). If the device does not exist, a new registration is created. Only clientId, metadata, and push.recipient fields are mutable; other fields are immutable. Returns the updated DeviceDetails object.
data.reasoning: Summary doesn't clearly indicate upsert behavior. Description lacks clarity on what happens when device doesn't exist and what is returned. Improved summary to reflect upsert semantics and added return value information.

message: Operation 'patchPushDeviceDetails' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details. Description doesn't explicitly state that this is a partial update (unlike PUT which replaces entirely) or what is returned. Added clarity on partial update semantics and return value for better AI agent understanding.

data.current_description: Specific attributes of an existing registration can be updated. Only clientId, metadata and push.recipient are mutable.
data.description_suggestion: Performs a partial update of an existing device registration. Only the provided fields are updated; unspecified fields remain unchanged. Only clientId, metadata, and push.recipient are mutable. Returns the updated DeviceDetails object.
data.reasoning: Description doesn't explicitly state that this is a partial update (unlike PUT which replaces entirely) or what is returned. Added clarity on partial update semantics and return value for better AI agent understanding.

message: Operation 'updatePushDeviceDetails' has semantic quality issues: unclear_purpose, vague_description, not_actionable, missing_output_details. The endpoint path suggests token generation/reset, but description doesn't clarify this. Summary mentions 'reset' but description doesn't explain what that means or why. data.current_summary: Reset a registered device's update token
data.current_description: Gets an updated device details object.
data.summary_suggestion: Generate a new device update token
data.description_suggestion: Generates a new update token for a registered device, enabling it to refresh its registration details. This token is used by the device to authenticate subsequent update requests. Returns the updated DeviceDetails object including the new update token.
data.reasoning: Current description 'Gets an updated device details object' is vague and doesn't explain the operation's actual purpose. The endpoint path suggests token generation/reset, but description doesn't clarify this. Summary mentions 'reset' but description doesn't explain what that means or why. Added clarity on token generation purpose, its use case, and return value for AI agent interpretability.

data.current_summary: Delete a registered device's update token
data.description_suggestion: Removes a device from push notification subscriptions by deleting its device details object. This operation unsubscribes the device from all channel subscriptions and prevents it from receiving further push notifications. Requires device identification parameters.

data.current_summary: List all channels with at least one subscribed device
data.current_description: Returns a paginated response of channel names.
data.description_suggestion: Retrieves a paginated list of all channel names that have at least one device subscribed to receive push notifications. Useful for discovering active push-enabled channels. Returns channel names only, sorted and paginated according to request parameters.
data.reasoning: Current description is minimal and doesn't explain the purpose or filtering criteria. AI agents need to understand that this returns only channels with active subscriptions, not all channels. Enhanced description clarifies the use case and response content.

message: Operation 'publishPushNotificationToDevices' has semantic quality issues: missing_output_details, missing_side_effects. Current description lacks clarity about what is returned and the side effects of the operation. AI agents need to understand that this directly targets devices rather than using channel subscriptions, and what the response indicates. Enhanced version clarifies the delivery mechanism and expected outcomes.

data.current_summary: Publish a push notification to device(s)
data.current_description: A convenience endpoint to deliver a push notification payload to a single device or set of devices identified by their client identifier.
data.description_suggestion: Delivers a push notification payload to one or more devices identified by device ID or client identifier. This is a convenience endpoint that bypasses channel-based subscriptions. Returns confirmation of delivery initiation. Notifications are queued for delivery to the specified devices.
data.reasoning: Current description lacks clarity about what is returned and the side effects of the operation. AI agents need to understand that this directly targets devices rather than using channel subscriptions, and what the response indicates. Enhanced version clarifies the delivery mechanism and expected outcomes.

message: Operation 'requestAccessToken' has semantic quality issues: vague_description, missing_input_details, missing_output_details. Current description is verbose and references external documentation rather than being self-contained. Missing clear input/output details. AI agents need concise information about what to send (TokenRequest or Signed TokenRequest) and what they receive (TokenDetails). Improved version is more actionable and focused on the operation's mechanics.

data.current_description: This is the means by which clients obtain access tokens to use the service. You can see how to construct an Ably TokenRequest in the [Ably TokenRequest spec](https://www.ably.io/documentation/rest-api/token-request-spec) documentation, although we recommend you use an Ably SDK rather to create a TokenRequest, as the construction of a TokenRequest is complex. The resulting token response object contains the token properties as defined in Ably TokenRequest spec. Authentication is not required if using a Signed TokenRequest.
data.summary_suggestion: Exchange token request for access token
data.description_suggestion: Exchanges a TokenRequest for an Ably access token. Accepts either a TokenRequest object or a Signed TokenRequest. Returns a TokenDetails object containing the access token, token expiration time, and associated capabilities. Authentication is not required when using a Signed TokenRequest. Use this endpoint to obtain short-lived tokens for client authentication.
data.reasoning: Current description is verbose and references external documentation rather than being self-contained. Missing clear input/output details. AI agents need concise information about what to send (TokenRequest or Signed TokenRequest) and what they receive (TokenDetails). Improved version is more actionable and focused on the operation's mechanics.
